text: The surgeon carefully dissects the posterior plane until reaching the suspected infiltration point of the mesorectal fascia. At this point, the dissection is directed deeper into the presacral fascia to achieve a clear circumferential resection margin, with precise technique to avoid nerve damage and ensure only the involved tissue is resected.

The surgeon completes the dissection by reaching the end of the mesorectal fascia involvement, returning to the good posterior mesorectal plane. The final pathological examination reveals a T-III tumor with no circumferential resection margin involvement, and radiation-induced fibrotic tissue is observed reaching the mesorectal fascia.
